blurb: Restoration Druid is the rating king of TBC 2v2 — Lifebloom stacks tick while you pillar-kite melee forever, and Cyclone turns every dead moment into offense. The playstyle is unique: pre-HoT, run, Cyclone, repeat. Gear-wise the ladder is unambiguous: PvP set, resilience everywhere, and the game becomes literally unlosable against certain comps.
statPriorityRationale: Resto Druid healing is HoT-based and keeps working while you run, which makes you the hardest healer to shut down — the gear leans resilience and stamina because Cyclone and mobility, not throughput, are what win your games.

faq.question: What rating are these Restoration Druids?
faq.answer: This snapshot covers 172 Restoration Druids between 2111 and 3111 rating (average 2564) on the TBC Classic arena ladder, refreshed from public leaderboard data.

faq.question: What is the most-used Restoration Druid PvP gear right now?
faq.answer: The highest-consensus picks are Idol of the Emerald Queen (98%), Bangle of Endless Blessings (84%), Veteran's Pendant of Salvation (83%). Anything above ~70% usage at this rating range is effectively mandatory.

faq.question: How much of the Restoration Druid BiS list is PvP gear vs raid gear?
faq.answer: 80% of the most-used slots are arena/honor items — for Restoration Druids the resilience on PvP gear beats raid alternatives almost everywhere. The exceptions are listed as alternatives per slot.

faq.question: What weapon do Restoration Druids use in arena?
faq.answer: Merciless Gladiator's Salvation is the main-hand of choice (65% of surveyed players).

faq.question: What gems do Restoration Druids socket for PvP?
faq.answer: Across all sockets, the most-used gems in this snapshot are +8 Spell Hit Rating, +9 Healing +3 Spell Damage and +4 Spirit, +18 Stamina & 5% Stun Resist. Socket bonuses are usually ignored unless they align with these.
